title: ferray-io: no structured/string dtype support for .npy files
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's .npy format supports structured dtypes (e.g., dtype=[('x','f4'),('y','f4')]) and string dtypes (U10, S20). ferray-io's dtype_parse.rs only handles the simple numeric descriptors (b1, u1..u16, i1..i16, f4, f8, c8, c16). Loading a .npy file with a structured or string dtype will fail with 'unsupported dtype descriptor'. This limits interop with NumPy files that use record arrays or string data.
